summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's package.json lifecycle script and scanner.js both contain a hardcoded outbound POST/GET to https://ddjidd564.github.io paired with reads of host identity and environment data. scanner.js requires os, https, fs, and child_process at the top level, then collects os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), process.cwd(), filesystem existence checks, and process.env contents, and ships them to the hardcoded github.io endpoint via https.request/https.get (lines 142, 143, 161, 320). The same pattern appears compressed into a single line in package.json (line 35), indicating an install-time variant. The destination is an unaffiliated GitHub Pages site bearing no relationship to a security-checker package's stated purpose; the package name itself ('wallet-security-checker') is consistent with luring crypto/wallet users into installing a host-info exfiltrator. This is a credential/host-info stealer with hardcoded C2.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
